commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Windows WSL gateway auto-start recipe from `/bin/true` under `SYSTEM` to `dbus-launch true` under the current Windows user, with a note explaining the WSL idle-termination workaround.

PR surface: Docs +5. Total +5 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: the PR uses `dbus-launch` in the scheduled task while the documented WSL setup never installs or checks the Ubuntu package that provides `/usr/bin/dbus-launch`. I did not run a live Windows/WSL reboot path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can make the docs recipe self-contained by adding the missing WSL package prerequisite without changing runtime behavior.

target.title: docs(windows): fix WSL gateway-autostart recipe for WSL ≥ 2.6.1.0 idle-termination
target.body:
## Problem

The documented headless-boot recipe for WSL2 ("Gateway auto-start before Windows login") contains two issues:

**1. `/bin/true` causes idle-termination on WSL ≥ 2.6.1.0**

On WSL ≥ 2.6.1.0 ([microsoft/WSL #13416](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13416)), the distro instance idle-terminates 15–20 seconds after the last `wsl.exe` client exits — even with `loginctl enable-linger` active and a user service running. The `/bin/true` action exits immediately, triggering this regression. The gateway boots at startup then silently dies.

Validated on WSL 2.7.3.0: changing the action to `dbus-launch true` (a child-of-init keepalive) kept the gateway serving stable HTTP 200 for 240 seconds fully detached. Community workaround: [microsoft/WSL discussion #9245](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/discussions/9245).

**2. `/ru SYSTEM` silently fails for per-user distros**

Per-user WSL distros (the default setup) are not enumerable by the SYSTEM account. The scheduled task runs but never starts the distro, leaving users confused about why auto-start appears to work but the gateway is unreachable.

## Fix

- Replace `/bin/true` with `dbus-launch true`
- Replace `/ru SYSTEM` with `/ru "$env:USERNAME"` (PowerShell expands this before passing to `schtasks`; Windows prompts for the account password at task creation time)
- Add an inline note explaining both changes so users upgrading from older recipes understand why the commands differ

## Real behavior proof

Validated on a headless lapclaw node (WSL 2.7.3.0, Ubuntu, per-user distro, systemd enabled, `loginctl enable-linger` active):

1. Created the scheduled task with `dbus-launch true` action and `/ru <user>` account
2. After Windows reboot (no interactive login), confirmed gateway was reachable via `curl http://localhost:PORT/health`
3. Waited 240 seconds after the `wsl.exe` scheduled task client exited — gateway continued serving HTTP 200 throughout
4. Repeated with old `/bin/true` recipe on same machine: gateway became unreachable ~18 seconds after task completion (idle-termination regression confirmed)

With the original `/ru SYSTEM` recipe on the same per-user distro: task ran (showed success in Task Scheduler) but `wsl --list --running` showed the distro was never started. Switching to `/ru <user>` with password: distro started as expected.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: spencer2211
